Alright, so welcome back to section 3. This is the lead. Now by now you've written your pre-headline qualifier, you've opened your question loop and it's time to write the lead, which in the context of coaching the conversion really is an expansion of the most important question. This function is to really unpack the importance of the question you just opened while really ramping up the stakes and having it answered. And when we break it down and get super granular, our lead is designed to coach resonance, agreement, anticipation through what I call head nod moments. You really want to list things that they could agree with or resonate with and resonance really is relatability on steroids. It's relating with something that is infused with emotion and when you do that you generate trust. Trust that you get it, which later creates trust that you can solve it. And you're also really shining a light on the stakes, creating that emotional need and having this one question answered as well as a knowledge gap and intellectual need to have it answered. So here's how to write it. So since the lead is an expansion of the question headline, the version of the template you used there is going to depend on the one you used in section 2. So if you used your if without would question loop in the last section, this is what your lead is going to look like. So you're going to start off with your headline, which is essentially going to say, why committing to X outcome is critical. You're going to outline the high level stakes in not committing to the outcome. You're going to get agreement on a series of what I call bleeding neck pain points, which you did in your 67 questions. And then you're going to take that agreement and turn it into the need to keep reading. And you're going to offer the promise or the moment of highest pleasure that they'll get in return for reading. Okay, so let's take a look at how the lead played out for Amy Porterfield's list builders lab. So we start with the headline. Here's why anything short of an enthusiastic head nod is costing you big time. And then the stakes every day with credit card in hand, thousands of your most valuable potential customers opt in to someone's email list with the hopes of having their most urgent problems solved. Question is, are they opting in to yours? And whether you've created an online course or have plans to create one in the future, the success of your service, coaching or training business rests heavily on your answer to this question. If you know without a doubt, and here's where we're going to get agreement that you're not reaching as many qualified, ready to buy subscribers as you should, or your current list building strategies feel random at best and so 2016 draining your energy while failing to attract a steady stream of leads that would make any of that hustle feel worth it. And then the promise because what follows could easily be the most pivotal, pivotal moment you'll hook back on a few weeks from now when your email list is flushed with a fresh stack of soon to be buyers who dig your stuff and aren't afraid to punch in their credit card details to prove it. So that is how it played out for version one. Now if you started with version one on your question loop, stop now, pause this video and go ahead and write your lead. But if you wrote version two, here's a template designed to expand on the difference maker. So if you guessed something they're doing wrong, something they're doing one, you'd be wrong. If you guessed something they're doing two, you'd be wrong. If you guessed something they're doing three, four and five, you'd still be wrong. And then declare the incompleteness of all those things and lead into the moment of highest tension. So let's quickly show you how this plays out in real time before it gets confusing. So this is back to the indestructible team by Soul Powered. If you guessed compensation package, you'd be wrong. If you guessed how many Myers-Briggs strengths finders or other competency test du jour you've put them through, you'd be wrong. And if you guessed how many Zoom check-ins, Slack chat rooms, or team retreats you've booked on your iCal, you'd still be wrong. Because while all these things have their place, they're only part of the equation. And unless you've engineered your team for massive contribution, rapid scale, and whoa, they can make money without me autonomy, the impact and income you're working tirelessly to scale will at best come at the expense of your cortisol levels, and at worse erupt into flames as you constantly flirt with an unrelenting sense of burnout. So those are the two versions of the lead. I invite you now to take a little break if you need it. If you still feel the energy, dive in, write your lead, and meet me in the next session where we will go deeper into the moment of highest tension. I'll see you there.
